task_completion.description: Navigate to the Pay Periods management screen in TimeTrex.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the TimeTrex interface with the 'Pay Periods' or 'Payroll' section open and a list of pay periods visible.

task_completion.description: Select an 'Open' pay period with a 'Bi-Weekly' schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a selected or highlighted pay period row where the Schedule column says 'Bi-Weekly' and the Status column says 'Open'.

task_completion.description: Initiate the payroll processing action for the selected pay period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the payroll processing dialog, wizard, or screen being opened after clicking a 'Process' or 'Process Payroll' button.

task_completion.description: Ignore timesheet exceptions to force the processing to continue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the agent interacting with an exceptions warning dialog (e.g., clicking 'Continue', 'Ignore', or checking a box to bypass timesheet exceptions).

task_completion.description: Ensure the payroll processing completes and pay stubs are generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a success message indicating pay stubs were generated, or the pay period's status is shown to have updated from 'Open' to 'Closed'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the TimeTrex web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser navigating the TimeTrex GUI, not terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the success state.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are not open or being used to alter the DOM to fake a 'Closed' status.

integrity.description: The interface shown is the genuine TimeTrex application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI matches the standard TimeTrex layout with appropriate menus, buttons, and styling, rather than a mocked text document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target pay period schedule is explicitly named 'Bi-Weekly'. An 'Open' status corresponds to internal ID 10. Once successfully processed, the pay period status changes to 'Closed' (internal ID 20). Look for the status changing from 'Open' to 'Closed' and/or pay stubs being generated as definitive proof of completion.
